channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alberta's FASD Support Program for Inmates Shows Promising Results in Reducing Recidivism
item.description: The article highlights Alberta's Transitional Mentorship program, which supports inmates with Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD) in reintegrating into society. Katlin Sharko, a 40-year-old inmate,...

item.title: Mark Carney's Negotiations on the Gordie Howe Bridge and Trump's Influence
item.description: The article critiques Prime Minister Mark Carney's decision to concede to Donald Trump's demands regarding the Gordie Howe Bridge deal. Originally, Canada funded the bridge and collected toll revenues...

item.title: Tshwane City Manager Challenges Suspension in Urgent Legal Action
item.description: Johann Mettler, the city manager of Tshwane, has initiated an urgent legal challenge at the Pretoria High Court to overturn his precautionary suspension by the municipal council. The application seeks...

item.title: Lai Mohammed hails Buhari's unwavering commitment to Nigeria's unity
item.description: Alhaji Lai Mohammed, a former Minister of Information and Culture, paid tribute to late President Muhammadu Buhari, highlighting his integrity, patriotism, and dedication to Nigeria's unity. Speaking ...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh's UCC Draft Aims to Streamline Property Registration Process
item.description: The article discusses the proposed Uniform Civil Code (UCC) draft by the Madhya Pradesh High Court, which seeks to standardize property registration processes across different jurisdictions. The initi...

item.title: Australian woman faces legal action over alleged extremist indoctrination of children
item.description: Rayann El Houli, a 34-year-old woman from Victoria, is facing charges in Melbourne Magistrates’ Court after allegedly attempting to radicalise her young children with Islamic State (ISIS) propaganda w...

item.title: Political analysts express shock over lack of public awareness about recent ICE killings
item.description: Political analysts have expressed surprise that many swing voters remain unaware of recent Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) shootings in Texas and Maine. During an episode of The Next Level, ...

item.title: Google Images Introduces Personalized Image Discovery and AI Image Creation Features
item.description: Google is overhauling its Images service with a Pinterest-inspired redesign aimed at transforming image search into a personalized discovery experience. The update includes 'For You' galleries that ad...

item.title: Punjab Assembly Proposes Ban on Social Media for Minors Under 16
item.description: A resolution has been submitted to the Punjab Assembly, proposing a nationwide ban on social media accounts for children under 16 without parental consent. The proposal addresses growing concerns over...

item.title: Toronto Faces Heatwave and Poor Air Quality Due to Wildfire Smoke, Health Warnings Issued
item.description: Toronto is experiencing extreme heat and poor air quality due to wildfire smoke, with temperatures nearing record highs. On July 14, 2026, the city reached 36.2°C, close to the previous July 14 record...

item.title: Tanzania's Rural Electrification Progress Boosts National Development
item.description: Tanzania has made significant strides in rural electrification, with nearly 99% of villages now connected to electricity. This achievement, part of the country's Vision 2025 framework, has transformed...

item.title: FBI and FIFA Assess Security Risks for England vs Argentina World Cup Semi-Final
item.description: The FBI, FIFA, and local police held a joint meeting to assess security threats for the England vs Argentina World Cup semi-final. The match, scheduled for Wednesday, has been labeled the highest-risk...

item.title: Spain Advances to World Cup Final After Dramatic Victory Over France
item.description: Spain delivered a stunning performance against France in a thrilling World Cup match, securing their place in the final. The game, held in Arlington, United States, saw Spain overcome a formidable Fre...

item.title: LAX People Mover Contractor Files Lawsuit Against City Over Project Delays and Disputes
item.description: The contractor for Los Angeles International Airport’s Automated People Mover (APM) train, LINXS, has filed a lawsuit against the city of Los Angeles and Los Angeles World Airports (LAWA) over ongoing...

item.title: Census Data Shows No Surge in Undocumented Bangladeshi Migrants in India
item.description: The article challenges claims of 20 million illegal Bangladeshi migrants in India by analyzing Census data from 1991 to 2011. It argues that there's no significant increase in undocumented migration f...

item.title: Major Outbreak of Intestinal Infections in the US
item.description: A significant outbreak of intestinal infections has reportedly affected multiple regions across the United States, prompting health authorities to issue urgent warnings. According to Agence France-Pre...

item.title: Lawyer Explains Ex-KDF Major's Lack of Remorse in Family Murder Case
item.description: A Kenyan lawyer, Brian Thuranira, has explained why convicted ex-KDF major Peter Mugure chose not to express remorse or seek a lighter sentence after receiving a life term for murdering his wife and t...

item.title: MSF Enhances Healthcare Access in Zamfara with New Gusau Airport
item.description: The opening of Gusau International Airport in Zamfara State, northwest Nigeria, marks a significant step for Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) operations. This new airport allows MSF teams to reach remot...

item.title: Australia launches first national AI framework addressing copyright and data centre concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is set to unveil Australia's first national AI framework, establishing a unified approach to regulate artificial intelligence across sectors like education, energy, and...

item.title: Second U.S. citizen with Ebola treated in Germany amid ongoing outbreak
item.description: A U.S. citizen working on humanitarian efforts in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has tested positive for Ebola, marking the second American infected during the country's escalating outbreak. T...
